infobox.key: South Wales Fire and Rescue Service
infobox.value: (**SWFRS**; [Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"): *Gwasanaeth Tân ac Achub De Cymru*) is the [fire and rescue service](/wiki/Fire_service_in_the_United_Kingdom "Fire service in the United Kingdom") covering the ten Welsh [principal areas](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Wales "Subdivisions of Wales") of [Blaenau Gwent](/wiki/Blaenau_Gwent "Blaenau Gwent"), [Bridgend](/wiki/Bridgend_(county_borough) "Bridgend (county borough)"), [Caerphilly](/wiki/Caerphilly_(county_borough) "Caerphilly (county borough)"), [Cardiff](/wiki/Cardiff "Cardiff"), [Merthyr Tydfil](/wiki/Merthyr_Tydfil "Merthyr Tydfil"), [Monmouthshire](/wiki/Monmouthshire "Monmouthshire"), [Newport](/wiki/Newport,_Wales "Newport, Wales"), [Rhondda Cynon Taf](/wiki/Rhondda_Cynon_Taf "Rhondda Cynon Taf"), [Torfaen](/wiki/Torfaen "Torfaen") and [Vale of Glamorgan](/wiki/Vale_of_Glamorgan "Vale of Glamorgan").
Covering most of what is commonly described as "[South East Wales](/wiki/South_East_Wales "South East Wales")". There are wider definitions of [South Wales](/wiki/South_Wales "South Wales").

["The South Wales Tri-Service Public Service and Joint Emergency Control Centre Tri-Service Centre Evaluation"](https://www.cardiff.ac.uk/news/view/1180033-the-south-wales-tri-service-public-service-and-joint-emergency-control-centre-tri-service-centre-evaluation). Cardiff University. 14 May 2018. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20210419093404/https://www.cardiff.ac.uk/news/view/1180033-the-south-wales-tri-service-public-service-and-joint-emergency-control-centre-tri-service-centre-evaluation) from the original on 19 April 2021. Retrieved 19 April 2021.

["National Framework for Fire and Rescue Services – Progress Report 2019"](https://gov.wales/sites/default/files/publications/2020-01/national-framework-for-fire-and-rescue-services.pdf) (PDF). Welsh Government. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20210419091901/https://gov.wales/sites/default/files/publications/2020-01/national-framework-for-fire-and-rescue-services.pdf) (PDF) from the original on 19 April 2021. Retrieved 19 April 2021.
["South Wales Fire & Rescue Service: "Fire Station Locations""](https://www.southwales-fire.gov.uk/app/uploads/2019/04/Station-Map-2020.pdf) (PDF). 2020. Retrieved 1 September 2023.

["South Wales Fire & Rescue Service: "Contact Us""](https://www.southwales-fire.gov.uk/contact-us/). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20230901181157/https://www.southwales-fire.gov.uk/contact-us/) from the original on 1 September 2023. Retrieved 1 September 2023.

South Wales Fire and Rescue Service

| [www.southwales-fire.gov.uk](https://www.southwales-fire.gov.uk/) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q7568770#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") | |

**South Wales Fire and Rescue Service** (**SWFRS**; [Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"): *Gwasanaeth Tân ac Achub De Cymru*) is the [fire and rescue service](/wiki/Fire_service_in_the_United_Kingdom "Fire service in the United Kingdom") covering the ten Welsh [principal areas](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Wales "Subdivisions of Wales") of [Blaenau Gwent](/wiki/Blaenau_Gwent "Blaenau Gwent"), [Bridgend](/wiki/Bridgend_(county_borough) "Bridgend (county borough)"), [Caerphilly](/wiki/Caerphilly_(county_borough) "Caerphilly (county borough)"), [Cardiff](/wiki/Cardiff "Cardiff"), [Merthyr Tydfil](/wiki/Merthyr_Tydfil "Merthyr Tydfil"), [Monmouthshire](/wiki/Monmouthshire "Monmouthshire"), [Newport](/wiki/Newport,_Wales "Newport, Wales"), [Rhondda Cynon Taf](/wiki/Rhondda_Cynon_Taf "Rhondda Cynon Taf"), [Torfaen](/wiki/Torfaen "Torfaen") and [Vale of Glamorgan](/wiki/Vale_of_Glamorgan "Vale of Glamorgan").

SWFRS was created in 1996 by the [Local Government (Wales) Act 1994](/wiki/Local_Government_(Wales)_Act_1994 "Local Government (Wales) Act 1994"), which reformed Welsh local government. It was created by a merger of the previous fire brigades of [Mid Glamorgan](/wiki/Mid_Glamorgan "Mid Glamorgan"), [South Glamorgan](/wiki/South_Glamorgan "South Glamorgan") and [Gwent](/wiki/Gwent_(county) "Gwent (county)"). It covers an area of around 1,100 square miles (2,800 km2) with a population of around 1.5 million.[[2]](#cite_note-stratplan2030-2)

The [fire authority](/wiki/Fire_authority "Fire authority") which runs the service is a [joint-board](/wiki/Joint-board "Joint-board") made up of councillors from the ten local authorities covered by the service.[[2]](#cite_note-stratplan2030-2)

Since October 2017, SWFRS has shared its control room with [Mid and West Wales Fire and Rescue Service](/wiki/Mid_and_West_Wales_Fire_and_Rescue_Service "Mid and West Wales Fire and Rescue Service") and [South Wales Police](/wiki/South_Wales_Police "South Wales Police") at the police headquarters in [Bridgend](/wiki/Bridgend "Bridgend"),[[3]](#cite_note-cfustudy-3)
an arrangement that is expected to save £1 million annually across both fire and rescue services.[[4]](#cite_note-gov2019-4)

## Fire stations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=South_Wales_Fire_and_Rescue_Service&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Fire stations")]

SWFRS operates 47 fire stations, of which 20 operate "wholetime" staffing (full-time firefighters) and 36 operate "retained" staffing (on-call firefighters), with some stations operating both crewing methods.[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5)

The service headquarters is located in Llantrisant, Glamorgan.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=South_Wales_Fire_and_Rescue_Service&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]
